item.title: Quick Note: Markdown Rendering Showcase
item.description: A compact post for testing common Markdown elements in the English article layout.
item.content:encoded:
This post is a compact Markdown rendering showcase for pocket-hugo-theme. It is meant to stress the article layout with the symbols and block styles people use most often.

Inline Formatting

You can check bold text, italic text, combined emphasis, inline code, and a standard link to Hugo.

You can also test superscript-like notation such as H2O, keyboard-style hints like Ctrl + K, and escaped characters such as *literal asterisks*.

Lists

Unordered List

  • Cover-first cards should still feel calm on mobile.
  • Metadata needs to stay readable at narrow widths.
  • Spacing matters as much as typography.

Ordered List

  1. Draft the post.
  2. Add a cover image.
  3. Review the mobile reading rhythm.

Task List

  • Add front matter
  • Add a local cover image
  • Replace demo copy with production writing

Quote

Good article themes disappear while you read.

Great ones also make browsing pleasant before you click.

Code Blocks

theme = "pocket-hugo-theme"
defaultContentLanguage = "en"
mainSections = ["posts"]

const article = {
  title: "Markdown Rendering Showcase",
  cover: "cover.webp",
  mobileReady: true,
};

Table

Element Why it matters
Headings Establish content rhythm
Lists Reveal spacing and indentation
Tables Stress overflow behavior on mobile
Code blocks Show padding, font, and wrapping

Image

The local bundle image should appear both as the cover and as an inline asset:

Horizontal Rule

Final Notes

If this article looks balanced on both desktop and phone, the theme is usually in a strong place for real essays, changelogs, notes, and documentation pages.

item.title: Notes from the Train
item.description: An English-only bundle demo with a compact travel diary voice.
item.content:encoded:
This demo post exists to verify a cover-driven page bundle in Pocket Hugo Theme. The image is declared explicitly in front matter so homepage cards, article headers, SEO metadata, and related-content blocks all point to the same asset.

To be, or not to be, that is the question. Whether ’tis nobler in the mind to suffer. The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.

A mobile-first card layout should still feel calm on a small screen. Short paragraphs, a visible cover, and restrained metadata usually make the reading flow easier to scan.

This paragraph is filler text on purpose. It gives the homepage, archives page, and taxonomy pages enough material to show varied summaries without depending on a real production article.

item.title: Publish, Pause, Re-read
item.description: An English-only bundle demo about slow publishing habits.
item.content:encoded:
This demo post exists to verify a cover-driven page bundle in Pocket Hugo Theme. The image is declared explicitly in front matter so homepage cards, article headers, SEO metadata, and related-content blocks all point to the same asset.

To be, or not to be, that is the question. Whether ’tis nobler in the mind to suffer. The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.

A mobile-first card layout should still feel calm on a small screen. Short paragraphs, a visible cover, and restrained metadata usually make the reading flow easier to scan.

This paragraph is filler text on purpose. It gives the homepage, archives page, and taxonomy pages enough material to show varied summaries without depending on a real production article.

item.title: Hamlet in a Small Window
item.description: An English-only bundle demo using a public-domain Shakespeare excerpt.
item.content:encoded:
This demo post exists to verify a cover-driven page bundle in Pocket Hugo Theme. The image is declared explicitly in front matter so homepage cards, article headers, SEO metadata, and related-content blocks all point to the same asset.

To be, or not to be, that is the question. Whether ’tis nobler in the mind to suffer. The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.

A mobile-first card layout should still feel calm on a small screen. Short paragraphs, a visible cover, and restrained metadata usually make the reading flow easier to scan.

This paragraph is filler text on purpose. It gives the homepage, archives page, and taxonomy pages enough material to show varied summaries without depending on a real production article.
